The user wants me to change the blue gradient to a "dracula pink gradient" in the `resizable-navbar.tsx` file. 

Looking at the file content provided, I can see the relevant section:

```javascript
const variantStyles = {
    primary:
        "shadow-[0_0_24px_rgba(34,_42,_53,_0.06),_0_1px_1px_rgba(0,_0,_0,_0.05),_0_0_0_1px_rgba(34,_42,_53,_0.04),_0_0_4px_rgba(34,_42,_53,_0.08),_0_16px_68px_rgba(47,_48,_55,_0.05),_0_1px_0_rgba(255,_255,_255,_0.1)_inset]",
    secondary: "bg-transparent shadow-none dark:text-white",
    dark: "bg-black text-white shadow-[0_0_24px_rgba(34,_42,_53,_0.06),_0_1px_1px_rgba(0,_0,_0,_0.05),_0_0_0_1px_rgba(34,_42,_53,_0.04),_0_0_4px_rgba(34,_42,_53,_0.08),_0_16px_68px_rgba(47,_48,_55,_0.05),_0_1px_0_rgba(255,_255,_255,_0.1)_inset]",
    gradient:
        "bg-gradient-to-b from-blue-500 to-blue-700 text-white shadow-[0px_2px_0px_0px_rgba(255,255,255,0.3)_inset]",
};
```

The "dracula pink" typically refers to a pink/purple color scheme. A dracula pink gradient would likely be something like:


- from-pink-500 to-purple-700 or
- from-[#ff79c6] to-[#bd93f9] (dracula theme colors)

I'll focus on updating the gradient to match the dracula pink color palette. The specific dracula theme pink is #ff79c6, which suggests a gradient that captures the essence of a dracula-inspired pink. I'll consider a gradient that transitions from a vibrant pink to a deeper, more intense shade.

The gradient will use the dracula theme's distinctive pink tones, creating a visually appealing and thematically consistent color transition.
